result.summary: The diff is comment-only, so I found no runtime regression or merge-blocking issue. I did find one low-severity maintainability problem: the new TODO points to a placeholder issue and describes removed retry behavior without a real tracker reference.

findings.title: Stale TODO uses a placeholder issue reference
findings.body: The added comment says `TODO(removed): retry on illegal move — see issue #N`, but `#N` is not a resolvable project issue and the wording says the retry logic was removed. This creates misleading follow-up work in the engine path: a future maintainer cannot tell whether there is an actual tracked requirement to reintroduce retry behavior or whether this is test-canary residue.
findings.recommendation: Remove the TODO, or replace it with a concrete local issue reference such as `.scratch/playlike-chess/issues/<NN>-...md` and a precise description of the intended behavior.

next_steps: I could not run the exact CI checks because `uv` is not installed in this environment.
Fallback direct Python checks also could not run because `pytest`, `ruff`, and `pyrefly` are not installed for the system Python.
